channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump taps former WWE CEO for education secretary and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Country Music Association Awards: who to watch
item.description: Post Malone and Morgan Wallen’s collaboration “I Had Some Help” could make both men big winners at Wednesday night's Country Music Association Awards.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON AP Entertainment Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Climate change goosed hurricane wind strength by 18 mph since 2019, study says
item.description: A new study says human-caused climate change made Atlantic hurricanes about 18 mph stronger in the last six years.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Western embassies in Kyiv shut due to Russian air attack threat after Biden policy shift
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The U.S. and some other Western embassies in Kyiv said that they would stay closed Wednesday for security reasons, with the American delegation saying it had received a warning of a potentially significant Russian air attack on the Ukrainian capital.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Booker Prize winner Richard Flanagan adds Baillie Gifford nonfiction prize to his trophy shelf
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Australian writer Richard Flanagan completed an unprecedented literary double on Tuesday, winning Britain’s leading nonfiction book prize a decade after being awarded the Booker Prize for fiction.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 2 Kentucky police officers shot during traffic stop on car reported stolen
item.description: Two police officers in Louisville, Kentucky, are recovering after they were shot while conducting a traffic stop on a stolen vehicle. A suspect inside the vehicle was also shot.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Marcos says Indonesia has agreed to send Filipino death-row drug convict back to the Philippines
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. said Wednesday that a deal has been reached for Indonesia to send back a Filipino death-row drug convict, who was nearly executed by firing squad but got a reprieve due to years of pleadings from Manila.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ AND NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ukraine has seen success in building clean energy, which is harder for Russia to destroy
item.description: This year, Russian missile attacks have knocked out about 50% of Ukraine's power generation, a major threat as winter looms.
item.dc:creator: Peter Prengaman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: North Korea sent more conventional weapons to Russia, South Korea says
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea recently supplied additional artillery systems to Russia to support its war efforts against Ukraine, while some of the thousands of North Korean troops deployed in Russia have begun engaging in combat, South Korea’s spy agency told lawmakers Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump has called for dismantling the Education Department. Here's what that would mean
item.description: Throughout his campaign, President-elect Donald Trump heaped scorn on the federal Department of Education, describing it as being infiltrated by “radicals, zealots and Marxists.”.
item.dc:creator: Annie Ma, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 5 things to know about Linda McMahon, Trump's pick for education secretary
item.description: If confirmed by the Senate, McMahon will be put in charge of heading up changes to Title IX and determining how student debt is handled.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Diddy hit with 5 new lawsuits, including alleged teen sexual assault
item.description: A woman says that at 17 years old, she attended a Fourth of July party in 2004 where Combs sexually assaulted her while she was unconscious.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Five more local, wild parrots found dead in Southern California
item.description: On Monday, Watts told NewsNation's KTLA that five more dead parrots were found in Temple City, plus another that was badly injured.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, NewsNation

item.title: Texas board advances plan to allow Bible material in elementary school lessons
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas' education board on Tuesday advanced a new Bible-infused curriculum that would be optional for schools to incorporate in kindergarten through fifth grades, one of the latest Republican-led efforts in the U.S. to incorporate more religious teaching into classrooms.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump to nominate professional wrestling mogul Linda McMahon to be education secretary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is nominating the billionaire professional wrestling mogul Linda McMahon to be secretary of the Education Department, tasked with overseeing an agency Trump has promised to dismantle.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and AMANDA SEITZ Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Reuters hires former Post and AP executive editor Sally Buzbee to lead US, Canada news operation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Sally Buzbee, the former executive editor of The Washington Post and The Associated Press, is joining Reuters as the news agency's top editor for the United States and Canada, the company said Tuesday. As news editor for the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Mattel releases doll honoring EGOT winner Rita Moreno
item.description: Toy maker Mattel is releasing a doll honoring Emmy, Grammy, Oscar and Tony (EGOT) winner Rita Moreno, a prominent Latina actor.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Texas offers Trump 1,400 borderland acres for deportation plans
item.description: Texas Land Commissioner Dawn Buckingham offered 1,402 acres in Starr County to the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Italian village offering $1 homes to Americans wanting to relocate after election
item.description: A small rural village in Italy is offering homes for as little as $1.06 after the 2024 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Survivors of LGBTQ+ club shooting say the deaths and trauma could've been avoided in lawsuits
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Adriana Vance collected herself in front of the cameras, two years to the day after her son was killed in the mass shooting at an LGBTQ+ nightclub in Colorado Springs, a murder that lawsuits allege could have been prevented by law enforcement and the club's owners.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fugitive killed, SWAT officer injured in hotel standoff situation: US Marshals
item.description: NewsNation affiliate WJW has confirmed US Marshals tracking a fugitive led to a police incident that closed I-77.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, NewsNation

item.title: Almost 100 House Democrats push for Gaetz Ethics report’s release
item.description: Nearly 100 House Democrats wrote to the House Ethics Committee, urging them to publish its report on former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) and their investigation into his alleged misconduct after President-elect Trump nominated him to serve as attorney general.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gabby Petito's family asks court to overturn police immunity in their civil case against Moab
item.description: The family of murder victim Gabby Petito is asking a Utah court to end the state's immunity for police officers as they attempt to move forward with a $50 million lawsuit against the Moab Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Scott Lewis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Prosecution rests in trial over death of Georgia nursing student Laken Riley
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — Prosecutors finished calling witnesses Tuesday in the trial of the man accused of killing Georgia nursing student Laken Riley, and the defense began presenting its case but quickly ran into snags. Jose Ibarra, 26, is charged with murder and other crimes in Riley’s February death.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Veteran, 98, finally receives medals earned in World War II
item.description: Rhode Island Senator Jack Reed hosted a special medal ceremony at his office in Cranston to present John DeLeo with his "overdue" military honors.
item.dc:creator: Catie McNeil, NewsNation

item.title: California governor will not make clemency decision for Menendez brothers until new DA reviews case
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom says he won’t make a clemency decision on the murder convictions of Erik and Lyle Menendez until newly elected Los Angeles County District Attorney Nathan Hochman reviews the case.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: FEMA administrator says she supports investigation of alleged Trump bias in relief efforts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The head of the Federal Emergency Management Agency told lawmakers Tuesday she has encouraged the agency's inspector general to review whether an employee was acting alone when directing workers helping hurricane victims not to go to homes with yards signs supporting President-elec
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 hospitalized after California theme park riders stranded mid-air
item.description: Two thrill-seekers were hospitalized Monday after they became stranded for more than two hours on a malfunctioning ride at Knott’s Berry Farm in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: Police seek witnesses, cell phone video after gunfire during a parade in New Orleans kills 2
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Deadly shootings at a signature New Orleans cultural event — a neighborhood “second-line” parade — left authorities pleading with witnesses to come forward with information or cell phone video while seeking to reassure the public that the weekend bloodshed was an aberration in a c
item.dc:creator: KEVIN McGILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trial begins for Florida man in human smuggling case after freezing deaths of Indian family at Canada-US border
item.description: FERGUS FALLS, Minn. (AP) — Federal prosecutors plan to argue at a Minnesota trial that two men were part of a criminal network stretching from India to Canada that smuggled families seeking better lives in the United States.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL GOLDBERG Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Beyoncé performing at halftime of NFL Christmas Day game on Netflix
item.description: Beyoncé is coming to your home on Christmas — provided you have Netflix and are tuning in to the Baltimore Ravens-Houston Texans game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Triathlete with terminal cancer completes Ironman championship in Hawaii
item.description: An Ironman is a test of endurance featuring a 2.4-mile swim, a 26.2-mile marathon run, and a 112-mile bike ride.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, WGN 9

item.title: The 9 GOP senators who could derail Trump’s Cabinet picks
item.description: Trump can afford three defections within the Senate GOP conference and still get his picks confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KRON4

item.title: Elon Musk is hiring for DOGE. Here's the type of people he wants to recruit
item.description: Just days after being tapped to co-lead the Department of Government Efficiency, Elon Musk is searching for "high-IQ" individuals to join the taskforce.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, Fox 5

item.title: Biden becomes the first sitting US president to visit the Amazon rainforest
item.description: Joe Biden on Sunday became the first sitting American president to set foot in the Amazon rainforest, as the incoming Trump administration seems poised to scale back the U.S. commitment to combating climate change.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: Trump attends UFC championship fight in New York, taking break from Cabinet picks
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump walked out to a roaring standing ovation just ahead of the start of the UFC pay-per-view card at Madison Square Garden on Saturday night, combining two things close to his heart: fierce battles inside the octagon and New York City.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Why you should throw away your black plastic cooking utensils
item.description: The tools you cook with may be introducing an unwanted ingredient into your family dinner: flame retardant.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WGN 9
